Description: Project management — door and window schedule coordination
SourceSection: Steps 2 and 5 — Owner confirmation and engineering coordination
Notes: PM coordination: Owner meetings for equipment dimensions (Step 2); interdisciplinary coordination with structural/electrical/mechanical (Step 5); schedule and progress tracking

Description: Cost estimating support — door and window schedule
Notes: Cost estimator input for hardware group costing; quantity verification of door/window units; preliminary pricing cross-checks for procurement planning

Description: Senior architect — code analysis and schedule development lead
SourceSection: Steps 1 through 6 — Code Analysis through Quality Review
Notes: Lead: Alberta Building Code analysis (occupancy, fire separations, egress, accessibility); fire rating determinations; hardware group definition; schedule completeness review; QA/QC oversight; professional seal responsibility

Description: Architect — schedule production and coordination support
SourceSection: Steps 3 through 5 — Tag List through Engineering Coordination
Notes: Production: door/window tag list development; schedule column population for 12+ door openings; cross-check against floor plans (DEL-001-02); structural coordination for opening sizes; electrical coordination for motorized doors (SOW-0060); renovation scope doors (SOW-0072, SOW-0073)

Description: BIM technician — schedule drawing sheet production
SourceSection: Steps 4 and 7 — Schedule Population and IFC Issue
Notes: BIM/CAD: schedule table layout on IFC drawing sheets; door/window mark placement on floor plans; cross-referencing between schedule and architectural drawings; final drawing formatting for IFC issue
